rationale: The citing note explicitly states it studies differentiated status-goods competition and “contrasts with conformity-exclusivity models,” while the cited paper is precisely a duopoly conspicuous-goods pricing model built on uniqueness/conformity social effects. Both papers are highly aligned on core constructs (conspicuous/status goods, social effects in utility, competitive pricing) and both include lab evidence, which makes this likely a substantive theoretical comparison/foundation rather than an incidental mention. With a direct DOI match to this specific prior model, this pair should be retained for full relation judgment.
